fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: labels: [{name: Blue Note, catalogNumber: BLP 4011}] (MB release a515abf5; Wikipedia: "released on Blue Note in 1959")

notes: albumDNA is fully correct and entity-matches: recorded Aug 25 1957 & Feb 25 1958, released Blue Note 1959, three tracks The Sermon/J.O.S./Flamingo (Wikipedia confirms verbatim; MB tracklist matches; keyTracks all match). Only the label field still ships 'Various' where MB/Wiki authoritatively give Blue Note. The year field 1987 disagrees with the matched MB release (1959) and the DNA's own '1959'; treated as an unverifiable later-reissue date (no evidence shows a 1987 issue, but reissues plausibly exist), not flagged as factual_error per the 'absence is not contradiction' rule.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: labels: [{name: Riverside, catalogNumber: VICJ-41820}] (MB embedded_mbid 1ab7ad48; Wikipedia: "recorded in 1960 and released on the Riverside label")

notes: albumDNA correct and entity-matched: recorded 1960, Riverside, hard bop, Toy/Lush Life/title track Spellbound (Wikipedia confirms verbatim). keyTracks all match MB tracklist. year 2007 equals the matched MB release date 2007-01-24 (a Japanese reissue; release-group first date 1960-11), so the year field is fine. Only the label field still ships 'Various' instead of Riverside.

No MB release returned; websearch resolved=true confirms album by Wayne Shorter on Blue Note, recorded Aug 1964, released 1965, Coltrane rhythm section McCoy Tyner/Reggie Workman/Elvin Jones, six Shorter originals incl title track and 'Yes or No'. albumDNA matches all of this. label 'Blue Note' and year 1965 correct. keyTracks match websearch tracklist exactly. wikipediaUrl https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JuJu_(album) confirmed by websearch as our entity's page.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: Recorded in September 1965 but released on the Blue Note label in early 1967 (Wikipedia); MB labels: [{name: Blue Note, catalogNumber: BLP 4222}]

The one checkable claim 'Recorded in September 1965' matches Wikipedia. Entity confirmed (Lee Morgan, Blue Note). keyTracks all match MB/Wiki. Only the label field still ships 'Various' vs Blue Note. year 2013 disagrees with matched MB release (1965) and Wikipedia release year (early 1967); treated as unverifiable later-reissue date, not a flagged contradiction.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: labels: [{name: Discos 605}, {name: Legacy}, {name: RCA}] (MB embedded_mbid 257bfe41, a 2009 reissue); the 1958 original was RCA Victor

notes: DNA generic boilerplate; the one checkable claim 'Recorded in 1958' matches the MB release-group first date 1958 and Wikipedia. Entity confirmed (Tito Puente). keyTracks all match MB tracklist. year 2009 equals the matched MB reissue release date 2009-04-21, so the year field is fine. The label field 'Various' is the weak spot: this single-artist album's matched reissue carries RCA/Legacy/Discos 605 and the 1958 original was RCA Victor -- 'Various' is not any of those (lower confidence because the reissue genuinely lists multiple labels, but it still misrepresents a single-artist album).

No MB release; websearch resolved=true confirms: guitarist Barney Kessel with Shelly Manne (drums) and Ray Brown (bass) on Contemporary, recorded Aug 1958, released 1958, second of five Poll Winners albums, standards Volare and Angel Eyes plus Kessel originals. albumDNA matches all of it. label 'Contemporary' and year 1958 correct. keyTracks match websearch tracklist. wikipediaUrl confirmed as our entity's page by websearch sources.

No MB release and no Wikipedia article; websearch resolved=true confirms via jazzdisco.org catalog: 'Song of the Wanderer' by Kid Ory (trombonist) on Verve, MGV-1014, released 1958, recorded 1957 in Los Angeles under the Norman Granz/Verve contract, with Marty Marsala (tpt), Darnell Howard (cl), Cedric Haywood (p). albumDNA matches all of this. label 'Verve' and year 1958 correct. keyTracks null (tracklist not enumerated on accessible sources; correctly left unfilled) -> n/a.

No MB release and no dedicated Wikipedia article; websearch resolved=true confirms: 'Wild Cat Blues' by Clarence Williams' Blue Five, recorded NYC July 30 1923, released Nov 1923 on OKeh (4925), b/w 'Kansas City Man Blues', featuring an early Sidney Bechet soprano-sax solo, written by Fats Waller & Clarence Williams, in the LoC National Recording Registry. albumDNA matches every claim (Blue Five, Williams/Waller authorship, early Bechet solo, NRR). label 'OKeh' and year 1923 correct. keyTracks (Wild Cat Blues, Kansas City Man Blues) match websearch tracklist. (It is a 78rpm single, not an album, but that is a cataloguing nuance, not a factual contradiction in the prose.)

No MB release and no dedicated Wikipedia article; websearch resolved=true confirms: solo piano by Art Tatum on Pablo (2310-723), original LP 1975, Norman Granz sessions Dec 1953-Jan 1955 Los Angeles. albumDNA matches every claim (first volume, Granz 1953-1955, Pablo 1975, standards Body and Soul/Love for Sale/Willow Weep for Me). label 'Pablo' and year 1975 correct. keyTracks match websearch tracklist. Note: the full catalog title is 'The Tatum Solo Masterpieces, Vol. 1'; ours 'Solo Masterpieces Vol. 1' is a shortened form (a title abbreviation, not a factual error).

DNA entity-matched and accurate: 'The Complete Billie Holiday on Verve 1945-1959 is a compilation of Billie Holiday's Verve recordings made between 1945 and 1959, released by Verve in 1993' matches MB exactly (full title 'The Complete Billie Holiday on Verve 1945-1959', embedded_mbid, label Verve, release/first date 1993-03-09, release-group secondaryTypes [Compilation]). label 'Verve' and year 1993 both correct. keyTracks (Body and Soul, Strange Fruit, I Cried for You, Fine and Mellow, He's Funny That Way, The Man I Love) all match the MB tracklist.

DNA entity-matched and accurate against Wikipedia verbatim: 1957 album, bassist Ray Brown and drummer Shelly Manne, first record with only bass and drums (no chordal instrument), tracks I'm an Old Cowhand/Come Gone/title cut, 1988 CD reissue added alternate takes. keyTracks all match MB/Wiki. label 'Original Jazz Classics' matches MB (labels OJC + Contemporary Records); year 1988 equals the matched MB (OJC reissue) release date and the DNA's own '1988 CD reissue'. Everything checkable is consistent.

DNA entity-matched and accurate: 'Released in 1966 on Atlantic' matches MB (release date 1966, label Atlantic); track program The Drum Also Waltzes/Nommo/title track/For Big Sid matches MB tracklist exactly; keyTracks all match. label 'Atlantic' is correct. The only soft spot is the year field 2004, which disagrees with the matched MB release (1966) and the DNA's own '1966'. No evidence shows a 2004 release, but a 2004 reissue plausibly exists, so per 'absence is not contradiction' the year cannot be called a factual_error -- it is unverifiable. (If the catalog intends 2004 as a reissue year it is unsupported; if it should be 1966 it is simply wrong; evidence cannot decide.)
